personality: 6 Glass / Luxe (with 7 Dark-First Utility typography for aviation aesthetic)
personality_one_liner: A cinematic, premium nightlife experience structured with the sharp, operational precision of an aviation departures board.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room for cinematic imagery, juxtaposed with tight, grid-aligned padding for aviation metadata (boarding passes, times).

do's: Use heavy glassmorphism over DJ and location photos for featured flight cards.
Use a frosted glass bottom tab bar so the dark event imagery bleeds through.
Apply dark tinted overlays (≥75% surface) under text on top of images for readability.
dont's: Don't apply glassmorphism to the final Boarding Pass ticket (that should look solid, tactile, and physical).
Don't use glass on input fields or utility forms.

screens.name: DeparturesBoard
screens.purpose: A live-feel, cinematic feed of upcoming flight-themed DJ events.

states.loading: A dark screen with pulsing metallic skeleton placeholders shaped like flight cards.
states.empty: No flights scheduled. Illustration: sleek hangar doors closed.

screens.layout: Vertical scroll. Top header mimics an airport 'DEPARTURES' board with Space Mono typography. Flight cards are edge-to-edge (or lg inset) large image-led modules. Image in background, linear gradient scrim, and sharp white tech/mono typography showing 'GATE', 'PILOT', and 'TIME' overlaid on the bottom.

screens.name: FlightDetail
screens.purpose: Expands the selected event into an immersive preview with seat class selection.

states.loading: Skeleton UI matching the hero image dimensions.

screens.layout: Parallax hero image of the destination/DJ. Below is the 'Booking Classes' segmented control (Economy, Business, First Class). Each class displays its perks (e.g., 'Premium Tables + Bottle') with Champagne brand tertiary highlights for First Class. Sticky 'Book Seat' CTA anchored to the bottom.

screens.name: Checkout
screens.purpose: Frictionless ticket reservation gathering basic passenger info.
screens.layout: Single column form. Input fields with dark surfaceTertiary backgrounds. A simulated 'swipe to pay' or premium gold 'Confirm Reservation' button at the bottom.

screens.name: MyTrips
screens.purpose: Digital wallet for the user's purchased boarding passes.

states.empty: A faded barcode illustration with text: 'No flights booked. Your passport is empty.'

screens.layout: A carousel or vertical list. CRITICAL: The boarding passes themselves use `surfaceInverse` (Off-white) with `onSurfaceInverse` (Pitch Black) mono typography. They must look like physical, premium printed flight tickets floating inside the dark app, complete with a barcode/QR block and dotted cut-lines.

hero_dj_1.alt: DJ performing in a dark, atmospheric club setting

destination_tokyo.alt: Dark neon Tokyo streets at night

bottle_service_first_class.alt: Premium champagne bottle service in a dark setting

instructions_to_main_agent: Aesthetic requirement: Merge '6 Glass / Luxe' (premium cinematic backgrounds, champagne gold accents) with '7 Dark-First Utility' (monospaced metadata, tight aviation grid alignment).
Font usage: Use Space Grotesk for the prominent event/destination names. Use JetBrains Mono for times, gates, flight numbers, and booking references to sell the 'aviation' feel.

Contrast flip on My Trips: The app is entirely dark mode, BUT the booked Boarding Passes must be styled using the `surfaceInverse` off-white color. They should look like bright, physical printed paper tickets sitting on the dark canvas.
Always apply a dark linear-gradient scrim (top to bottom, transparent to ≥75% surface) over hero images so the white and gold text overlaid on them remains readable.
Form inputs should not float. Labels above inputs. Use surfaceTertiary for input backgrounds, completely borderless.
Use standard safe-area views. Do not let content bleed under the notch unmanaged.
